tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment, and low total CO₂, which may indicate mild metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), raising concern for renal dysfunction and possible dehydration or metabolic disturbance. HbA1c is low, indicating no chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend clinical correlation for renal function, hydration status, and possible further nephrological evaluation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
